title: papaver rhoeas var. erraticum
intro: Papaver rhoeas var. erraticum, commonly known as the Shirley poppy, is a perennial plant species that is a variant of the Papaver rhoeas, a wild poppy native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is known for its vibrant pink to red flowers, which are rich in medicinal compounds, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. The medicinal properties of Papaver rhoeas var. erraticum include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as pain, insomnia, and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
